summary: Installer pairing finalization now distinguishes reuse vs refresh for existing hosts, keeps Telegram claim semantics intact, and is covered by fresh targeted plus full bootstrap verification.

checks.name: pairing-state-machine
checks.detail: Installer finalization now distinguishes no-local-host auto-request, existing-host probe, paired/active reuse, refresh-required reissue, and manual fallback through shared pairing logic.

checks.name: telegram-claim-boundary
checks.detail: Telegram `/pair` remains the only host-claim boundary; installer automation is limited to probing state and requesting or refreshing the pairing code.

checks.name: successful-auto-request-paths
checks.detail: Fresh runtime coverage proves no-state auto-request, existing `registering` refresh, existing `active` reuse, invalid-token blocking, and existing-host `probe-unavailable` manual fallback, and no contradictory manual `pnpm daemon:pair` remains after successful auto-request.

checks.name: verification-commands
checks.detail: `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap run build`, `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap run typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap run lint`, targeted pairing runtime tests, and `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap run test` all passed on 2026-04-19.

residualRisks: Normal residual risk remains that future edits could change installer wording or finalization classification, but the critical pairing branches are now regression-covered.
